notes: Core T cell markers (CD3 complex) combined with CD4 define this population. CCR7/TCF7 further distinguish naive from memory.
Cytotoxic T cells. GZMB/PRF1 mark effector function. Exhausted CD8+ T cells additionally express PDCD1, HAVCR2, TIGIT.
FOXP3 is the master regulator. IL2RA (CD25) co-expression with FOXP3 is the gold standard. Highly enriched in tumors.
CD56 (NCAM1) is canonical. CD56dim (FCGR3A+) are cytotoxic; CD56bright (FCGR3A-) are cytokine producers. No TCR expression.
CD20 (MS4A1) and CD19 are the clinical standard. CD79A/B are part of the B cell receptor complex.
Terminally differentiated B cells. Note: CD19/CD20 are downregulated. PRDM1 (Blimp1) is the master regulator.
Classical monocytes (CD14++CD16-). S100A8/S100A9 are highly specific. Non-classical monocytes are CD14+CD16+ (FCGR3A+).
Tissue macrophages vary greatly by location. TREM2+ macrophages are a tumor-associated subset. C1Q complex is broadly reliable.
cDC1: CLEC9A+XCR1+. cDC2: CD1C+FCER1A+. pDC: LILRA4+CLEC4C+. IRF8 marks cDC1 lineage commitment.
Tryptase (TPSAB1/TPSB2) is the gold standard. CPA3 is highly specific. KIT (CD117) alone is insufficient — also marks other cells.
FCGR3B (CD16b) is neutrophil-specific. MPO/ELANE are granule proteins. Hard to capture in standard scRNA-seq due to low RNA content.
EpCAM and cytokeratins (KRT8/18/19) are canonical. CDH1 (E-cadherin) marks epithelial identity. Loss of CDH1 indicates EMT.
Formerly Clara cells. SCGB1A1 (CC16) is the gold standard lung-specific marker. Found in bronchioles.
AT1 cells are large, flat, cover 95% of alveolar surface. AGER (RAGE) and PDPN (podoplanin) are canonical.
Surfactant protein C (SFTPC) is the gold standard AT2 marker. AT2 cells are the lung stem cell population.
Albumin (ALB) is canonical. Zonation exists: pericentral (CYP2E1+) vs periportal (PCK1+). HNF4A is the master TF.
Biliary epithelial cells. KRT19+KRT7+ALB- distinguishes from hepatocytes. SOX9 marks biliary progenitors.
Absorptive cells lining the small intestine. FABP1/2 and brush border enzymes (SI, LCT) are canonical.
MUC2 is the gold standard mucin for intestinal goblet cells. TFF3 (trefoil factor 3) is highly specific.
Collagen genes (COL1A1/1A2/3A1) are canonical but not exclusive. FAP marks cancer-associated fibroblasts. ACTA2 marks myofibroblasts.
CD31 (PECAM1) and VE-cadherin (CDH5) are the gold standard. PLVAP marks fenestrated endothelium. FLT1 marks tip cells.
RGS5 is highly specific. PDGFRB alone insufficient — also marks fibroblasts. ACTA2 marks contractile pericytes/smooth muscle.
MYH11 is the most specific smooth muscle marker. ACTA2 (αSMA) is shared with myofibroblasts and pericytes.
Adiponectin (ADIPOQ) and leptin (LEP) are gold standard. PLIN1 marks lipid droplet-associated protein. PPARG is the master TF.
NeuN (RBFOX3) is the gold standard post-mitotic neuron marker. SYP/SYT1 mark synaptic vesicles. DCX marks immature neurons.
GFAP is canonical but varies by region and reactivity. ALDH1L1 is more broadly expressed. AQP4 marks endfeet. Reactive astrocytes upregulate VIM.
Myelin genes (MBP, MOG, PLP1) are highly specific. OLIG2 marks both OPC and oligodendrocytes. CNP marks mature myelinating cells.
NG2 (CSPG4) is canonical OPC marker. PDGFRA drives OPC proliferation. Distinguished from mature oligodendrocytes by absence of MBP/MOG.
P2RY12 and TMEM119 are highly CNS-specific homeostatic markers, lost in disease-associated microglia (DAM). TREM2 marks DAM subset.
LGR5 is the definitive intestinal stem cell marker. OLFM4 is highly expressed and more stable than LGR5 in sequencing data.
CD34 is broadly expressed on progenitors. True HSCs are CD34+CD38-CD90+CD45RA-. MLLT3 and SPINK2 are more specific in scRNA-seq.
FAP is the most specific CAF marker. Multiple CAF subtypes exist: myoCAF (ACTA2+), iCAF (IL6+), apCAF (MHC-II+). Context-dependent.
SPP1+ TAMs are associated with poor prognosis. TREM2+ TAMs are immunosuppressive. M1/M2 polarization is a simplification — TAM states exist on a continuum.
Co-expression of multiple inhibitory receptors defines exhaustion. TOX is a key TF. TCF7+ progenitor exhausted cells can respond to checkpoint blockade.
Hemoglobin genes (HBB, HBA1/2) are definitive. Often considered contamination in non-blood tissues. GYPA (glycophorin A) is highly specific.
PF4 (CXCL4) and PPBP (CXCL7) are platelet-derived. GP1BA (CD42b) is the von Willebrand factor receptor.
